title: rubus styriacus
intro: Rubus styriacus, also known as the Styrian raspberry or Austrian bramble, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family that is native to the mountainous regions of Central and Eastern Europe. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Rubus styriacus include its ability to help reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and support the immune system. Rubus styriacus can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture for its medicinal purposes, and it is also used in herbal remedies to treat various health conditions.
